body:
Mijo, I keep humming that melody you sang for me Tuesday night. The one in the minor key with the line about the highway and the kitchen light. I told your Abuelita about it and she got quiet in that way she does when something hits her. She wants to know if it's about your Tío Beto's old truck. Is it ever going to be on an album or does it just disappear like the others? I never understand how that part works.

Also I made tamales this weekend if you want me to send some with Carlos next time he passes through. Let me know.
snippet: Mijo, I keep humming that melody you sang for me Tuesday night. The one in the m...
subject: That song you played me on the phone

body:
Mamá. Tell Abuelita it's not about Tío Beto's truck — but tell her she's not entirely wrong either. That's how these things work, they're never about one thing.

Derek's got it on hold right now for a project he's pitching, so it hasn't been cut yet but it hasn't died. Hold means somebody's at least standing in the doorway. Vanessa thinks there might be a placement if the project moves, but I've learned not to count anything until the check clears and sometimes not even then.

Say yes to the tamales. Tell Carlos don't let them freeze this time.
snippet: Mamá. Tell Abuelita it's not about Tío Beto's truck — but tell her she's not ent...
subject: Re: That song you played me on the phone

body:
Andrew. It has been six weeks. SIX. I know you've been in the studio because I saw Derek post that reel of the tracking session and you looked like you were having a whole spiritual experience over a tambourine part, but that does not count as seeing you.

I'm off Thursday evening and Sunday is wide open. Thinking dinner at my place — I made that black bean soup last week and I'm already planning the next round. Bring nothing except yourself and maybe that story about the hold that fell through that you texted me three sentences of and then just STOPPED.

Let me know what works.
snippet: Andrew. It has been six weeks. SIX. I know you've been in the studio because I s...
subject: ok when are we actually doing this

body:
Six weeks is embarrassing and I accept full blame. That tambourine reel was Derek being messy — I told him not to post mid-session but he does what he wants.

Thursday's tight, I've got a vocal date at two and those tend to bleed. Sunday works though. I'll come to you. And I'll tell you the whole hold story in person because it needs hand gestures and probably a glass of wine to land right — it's equal parts funny and bleak, very much like a Townes Van Zandt song if Townes had a publishing rep named Vanessa.

What time? I do yoga Sunday mornings so I'm free from around nine onward, or I can come midday, whatever suits you.
snippet: Six weeks is embarrassing and I accept full blame. That tambourine reel was Dere...
subject: Re: ok when are we actually doing this

body:
Midday works perfectly — come around noon, soup will be ready by then and I'll have cornbread too so don't eat beforehand.

And I KNEW that hold story was going to require wine. You cannot tease 'it's complicated' over text and then go silent for a week, that is genuinely cruel. Bring your whole chest.

See you Sunday. Tell Rosa I said hello.
snippet: Midday works perfectly — come around noon, soup will be ready by then and I'll h...
subject: Re: ok when are we actually doing this

body:
Mijo, your abuelita has been asking about you every single day this week. I told her you were in the studio and she said, "siempre en el estudio" like I personally put you there.

Carlos is driving up from San Antonio Saturday and staying through Sunday. I'm doing the red rice and the pork ribs you like — the ones with the chile ancho. If you can get here by six o'clock Sunday we can all eat together while it's still warm.

Let me know. Abuelita will sleep better.

— Mamá
snippet: Mijo, your abuelita has been asking about you every single day this week. I told...
subject: Sunday dinner at the house — you coming?

body:
Mamá. Yes. I'm there.

I've got a tracking session Thursday afternoon with Derek that'll probably run long — he always says three hours, means six — but I'm clear by Friday evening. I'll drive down Saturday morning, leave here by seven so I miss the 65 traffic.

Tell Abuelita I'm bringing her the pan dulce from the bakery on Nolensville she liked last time. And tell Carlos to actually show up, not just his ETA.

— Drew
snippet:
Mamá. Yes. I'm there.

I've got a tracking session Thursday afternoon with Derek...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house — you coming?

body:
Good. I already told Abuelita you were coming so there's no backing out now, she's planned her whole week around it.

Carlos says he's bringing the kids, so it'll be a full house. If you can stop at the HEB on Culebra and pick up a bag of the dried pasilla your abuelita likes — the ones in the small red bag, NOT the big one — that would save me a trip. She says the ones I buy are wrong. They're the same ones. They're always the same ones.

Dinner at six. Don't be your father.

— Mamá
snippet: Good. I already told Abuelita you were coming so there's no backing out now, she...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house — you coming?

body:
Small red bag. Got it. I'll text you a photo in the aisle so we don't have the pasilla situation of 2019.

Happy to see the kids — tell Carlos they better be ready to hear some new songs because I've been working on something that might be the best thing I've written since that cut Vanessa shopped last spring. Or it's terrible. Hard to tell this close to it. Either way they have to listen.

See y'all Saturday. Give Abuelita a kiss for me and tell her I've been warming up every morning.

— Drew
snippet: Small red bag. Got it. I'll text you a photo in the aisle so we don't have the p...
subject: Re: Sunday dinner at the house — you coming?

body: Mijo, your Abuelita has been asking about you every time I call her. I'm thinking of driving her up from San Antonio this weekend and making a whole day of it — carnitas, rice, the works. Carlos says he can make it if we do Sunday. Can you come? I know you have your music things but this would mean a lot to her. She keeps saying you sound like your grandfather on the radio. Let me know soon so I can plan the food.
snippet: Mijo, your Abuelita has been asking about you every time I call her. I'm thinkin...
subject: Sunday at the house

body:
Mamá. Yes. I've got a tracking session Friday afternoon at Bluebird Track but it should wrap by six — I can be on the road by seven, sleep at the house Saturday, and have the whole Sunday free.

Tell Abuelita I'll sing her something in the kitchen if she teaches me that thing she used to hum while she made tamales. I've been trying to figure out what song that was for fifteen years.

Do you need me to bring anything? I can stop at H-E-B on the way down if you need — just text me the list before I hit the road.
snippet: Mamá. Yes. I've got a tracking session Friday afternoon at Bluebird Track but it...
subject: Re: Sunday at the house

body:
Ay, mijo, she is going to cry. In a good way. I'll tell her tonight when I call.

Yes — bring the Jarritos if you're stopping. The mandarina ones, not the lime, your brother always picks the lime and nobody else likes it. And maybe a bag of masa harina, I don't trust the store here.

Carlos is bringing the kids so it'll be loud. Fair warning. But the good kind of loud.
snippet: Ay, mijo, she is going to cry. In a good way. I'll tell her tonight when I call....
subject: Re: Sunday at the house

body:
Mandarina, got it. Carlos and his lime choices — man has no range. Tell him I said that.

Good loud is fine. I spend half my life in a booth with headphones on, I can handle a few sobrinos running circles around me. Honestly might be the most alive I feel all week.

I'll text when I leave Nashville. If the session runs late I'll still make it by midnight — don't wait up but leave the back door unlocked.
snippet: Mandarina, got it. Carlos and his lime choices — man has no range. Tell him I sa...
subject: Re: Sunday at the house

body:
Back door will be open and there will be leftover caldo on the stove. You know where the bowls are.

Your Abuelita wants me to tell you she's been listening to that song you sent her in March. She plays it every morning. She said it sounds like home. I don't know which song, she wouldn't tell me, but I think you do.

Drive safe, mijo. Reza un poco on the highway.
snippet: Back door will be open and there will be leftover caldo on the stove. You know w...
subject: Re: Sunday at the house

body:
Mijo, I need to know if you're coming this Saturday for Abuelita's birthday dinner. I'm making her pozole and the tamales she likes, and Carlos is driving down from El Paso. She keeps asking if you'll sing something for her — you know how she gets. Just let me know by Thursday so I can count heads and tell Tía Lupe how many plates.

Also I saw on Facebook you have a show next week? Why do I always find out from Facebook, Andrew.
snippet: Mijo, I need to know if you're coming this Saturday for Abuelita's birthday dinn...
subject: Abuelita's birthday dinner — this Saturday

body:
Mamá, I'll be there. Session wraps Friday afternoon and Derek doesn't need me back in til Monday, so I can drive down Saturday morning and stay the night.

Tell Abuelita I'll bring my guitar and we'll do all her songs — the ones she and Abuelo used to dance to in the kitchen. She always cries at La Barca de Oro and then acts like she's fine. I live for it.

And yes, the show is at The Listening Room next Thursday. I was going to tell you on our call Wednesday, I promise. Put it on your refrigerator.
snippet: Mamá, I'll be there. Session wraps Friday afternoon and Derek doesn't need me ba...
subject: Re: Abuelita's birthday dinner — this Saturday

body:
Andrew. I know you had sessions all week and your body is probably one giant knot right now, but Radnor Lake Sunday morning. Me, you, maybe Cole if he can pull himself away from his sourdough situation. I'm thinking 8am start so we beat the crowd and you can still make your afternoon thing.

Say yes. You need actual sunlight that isn't stage lighting.
snippet: Andrew. I know you had sessions all week and your body is probably one giant kno...
subject: Sunday hike — you in or you hiding in the studio again

body:
I'm in. Yoga's at 6:30 so I'll be loose by the time we hit the trailhead. 8am works — I can leave straight from Sunrise Flow, just tell me which lot.

Tell Cole leave the bread at home. Last time he brought a whole loaf and we fed half of it to a duck and I still feel bad about that.
snippet: I'm in. Yoga's at 6:30 so I'll be loose by the time we hit the trailhead. 8am wo...
subject: Re: Sunday hike — you in or you hiding in the studio again

body:
Spillway lot off Otter Creek Road, there's usually parking if you're not rolling in at 9. Cole says he's coming and specifically says he is NOT bringing bread, which means he is absolutely bringing bread.

Also I want to hear this new song you've been cryptic about on Instagram. You post one line of a lyric and then nothing for four days — that's cruel and you know it.
snippet: Spillway lot off Otter Creek Road, there's usually parking if you're not rolling...
subject: Re: Sunday hike — you in or you hiding in the studio again

body:
Andrew, quick one before I lose the thread. The Carly Thornton mechanical statement finally posted and the line for your cut on the album track reads lower than what we projected. I think the streaming tier got miscoded on their end, not ours. I have already flagged it with their admin and I am copying you so you have it in writing. Nothing for you to do yet, just keeping you in the loop the way you asked me to after the last mess. I will chase it down and report back by Friday.

Vanessa
snippet: Andrew, quick one before I lose the thread. The Carly Thornton mechanical statem...
subject: Carly Thornton statement looks off

body:
Vanessa, thank you for putting it in writing. You already know why that matters to me. Friday is fine, no rush as long as the trail does not go cold. If it is their coding error then I would rather it get fixed quietly than turn into a thing, I do not have the stomach for another fight over a few hundred dollars.

While I have you: Derek wants me back Tuesday at two to re-cut the second verse on Rio Grande Prayer. If you need anything from me before the Cody hold paperwork moves, tell me now so I can batch it.

Andrew
snippet: Vanessa, thank you for putting it in writing. You already know why that matters ...
subject: Re: Carly Thornton statement looks off

body:
Drew, it is your favorite engineer. Heads up that the vocal chain you like is staying patched in the B room because A is getting the console recap this week, so when you come Tuesday you are in B. Same mics, I moved your U87 and the Distressor over already. Bring the lyric sheets printed, the wifi in B has been dropping and I do not want you squinting at your phone between takes again.

Also Derek says if you are early we can grab tacos at the truck on Gallatin first. He is buying, which has never once happened, so come early.

Terrell
snippet: Drew, it is your favorite engineer. Heads up that the vocal chain you like is st...
subject: Tuesday session, you are in the B room

body:
Terrell, B room is fine, honestly I sing better in there anyway, the A room ceiling does something weird to my top end. Thank you for moving the chain, you are the only reason these sessions run on time and we both know it.

Derek buying tacos is a historic event and I will be there early to witness it. Print the lyric sheets, copy that. I will bring the new second verse, I rewrote the turnaround line on the drive home Sunday and I think it finally lands. See you Tuesday.

Drew
snippet: Terrell, B room is fine, honestly I sing better in there anyway, the A room ceil...
subject: Re: Tuesday session, you are in the B room

body:
Hermano, I am driving up the 26th with the kids, leaving El Paso early so we hit Nashville by night. Diego has not stopped talking about you teaching him guitar since Thanksgiving so clear an afternoon. Sofia just wants to see the studio, do not let Derek scare her.

Mamá is sending tamales and she swears they are frozen correctly this time, she made me promise to tell you that exact sentence. Text me your address again, I lost it when my phone died in Junction.

Carlos
snippet: Hermano, I am driving up the 26th with the kids, leaving El Paso early so...
subject: Driving up the 26th

body:
Carlos, yes, come, the door is open. I will clear the 27th for Diego, tell him to bring the little Yamaha and we will get his G chord to actually ring this time. Sofia can come to Copperline, Derek is a teddy bear once you get past the beard, I will tell Terrell to let her push a fader.

Sending you the address now. Drive safe through the hill country, the deer come out at dusk on the 290. And tell Mamá I believe her about the tamales, but I am checking the box at the door regardless.

Drew
snippet: Carlos, yes, come, the door is open. I will clear the 27th for Diego, tell him t...
subject: Re: Driving up the 26th

body:
Drew, it is DT. Rough mix of Rio Grande Prayer with the new second verse is up on the drive, link below. I pushed the acoustic the half dB Vanessa wanted, do not tell her she was right. Give it a listen on real speakers, not your phone, and tell me if the harmony stack in the last chorus is too thick. I can thin it but I kind of love it fat.

If you sign off we send it to Vanessa for the Carly Pearce camp Friday. No pressure, only your whole year riding on it. Kidding. Mostly.

DT
snippet: Drew, it is DT. Rough mix of Rio Grande Prayer with the new second verse is up o...
subject: Rio Grande Prayer rough mix is up

body:
DT, just played it three times on the JBL with the lights off, the way it is supposed to be heard. The fat harmony stack stays, do not you dare thin it, that is the whole sound of the song, it sounds like a family singing in a kitchen and that is the point. Maybe a hair less reverb on the lead in the verse so the words sit closer, but that is the only note.

Send it to Vanessa Friday. And thank you, man. Whatever happens with the hold, the song is right, and that is the part nobody can take.

Drew
snippet: DT, just played it three times on the JBL with the lights off, the way it is sup...
subject: Re: Rio Grande Prayer rough mix is up
